Structural System Summary:

Primary system: Wood-framed bearing walls with engineered wood I-joist floors. CMU shear walls at stair/elevator cores. Lateral system: Wood shear walls and CMU shear walls. Floor system: Engineered wood I-joists with 3/4" T&G sheathing and gypcrete topping. Some limited areas of structural steel framing (W-shapes). Roof system: Pre-engineered wood trusses with 3/4" T&G sheathing. Foundation interface: Concrete shallow spread footings and slab on grade. Special conditions: Steel framed canopies and an exterior trellis structure. Steel lintels in CMU and steel headers in wood framing.
Approximate Tonnage Estimate:

Primary steel: ~9.5 tons Secondary / misc: ~1.5 tons Total project: ~11.0 tons Confidence: Medium Basis: Counted from framing plans S1.02, details on S3.05, S3.06, and schedules on S0.02. High amount of repetition helps confidence, but lengths are estimated from grid dimensions.

Summary line: 1 Critical | 3 Major | 2 Minor | Total: 6 issues
RFI-001 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.01 — Weld Filler Metal Specification Priority: Critical Blocking: Yes
Question: Per S0.01 General Structural Notes, all welding is to conform to AWS D1.1. Please confirm the required weld filler metal strength (e.g., 70ksi).
Recommended Answer Format: Written confirmation of filler metal strength (e.g., "Confirm 70ksi electrodes are required.")
--- RFI-002 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.01, S3.05, S3.06 — Finish for Exterior Steel Priority: Urgent Blocking: No

Question: The canopy and trellis steel on sheets S3.05 and S3.06 are exposed to weather. General Note on S0.01 specifies shop primer U.N.O. Please confirm the required finish for all exterior structural steel (e.g., Galvanizing per ASTM A123, or a specific paint system).
Recommended Answer Format: A written specification or reference to an architectural specification for the required finish system.
--- RFI-003 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.02, S3.06 — Anchor Bolt Projection and Grout Thickness Priority: Urgent Blocking: No

Question: The Steel Column Schedule on S0.02 and detail 2/S3.06 provide anchor bolt embedment but do not specify the required projection above top of concrete. Please provide the required projection and the design grout thickness for all column bases.
Recommended Answer Format: Written confirmation of required projection (e.g., "Provide 3" projection") and grout thickness (e.g., "Design for 1-1/2" grout").
--- RFI-004 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S0.01 — Delegated Connection Design Loads Priority: Urgent Blocking: Yes

Question: Per General Note 7 on S0.01, all steel-to-steel connections are to be designed by the fabricator's engineer. Please provide a complete set of connection forces (axial, shear, moment) for all steel connections to allow design to proceed.
Recommended Answer Format: A supplementary drawing or table listing factored loads for each unique connection type shown on the drawings.
--- RFI-005 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S1.01 — Column Orientation Priority: Standard Blocking: No
Question: Please provide the orientation for columns C4 (HSS8x4) and C5 (HSS9x5) on the Foundation Plan S1.01 relative to the column grid lines.
Recommended Answer Format: A marked-up plan sketch showing the correct orientation.
--- RFI-006 To: Taylor & Viola Structural Engineers Re: S3.05 — Scope of Light Gauge Steel Joists Priority: Standard Blocking: No

Question: Details on sheet S3.05 show 600S200-54 light gauge steel joists framing between HSS18x6 beams. Please clarify if these light gauge members are to be supplied and installed by the structural steel fabricator.
Recommended Answer Format: Written confirmation of scope responsibility (e.g., "Light gauge joists are by steel fabricator" or "Light gauge joists are by others").
Group RFIs:

Critical RFIs (must answer before modeling starts): RFI-001 Urgent RFIs (answer within first week of modeling): RFI-002, RFI-003, RFI-004 Standard RFIs (answer before drawing release): RFI-005, RFI-006
